titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the W. Somerset Maugham Collection
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke Staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 4114914.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

conventiondeclaration.abbr: beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual
conventiondeclaration.citation: translation missing: en.enumerations.resource_finding_aid_description_rules.beinecke manuscript unit archival processing manual

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 6, 2020 at 04:23

did.unittitle: W. Somerset Maugham collection

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Contains letters from Maugham to various people, including William Morris Colles, Ada Leverson, Charles Hanson Towne, and Donald Goddard Wing; manuscript and proof versions of writings by Maugham, including "Ashenden, or the British agent", "Lady Frederick", and "On a Chinese Screen"; and an etching of Hugh Walpole hitting Elinor Mordaunt in the jaw.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Ongoing collection of documents acquired by gift and purchase from various sources. Type of accession (gift or purchase) and date of acquisition is noted in the box-and-folder list. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The W. Somerset Maugham Collection is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: W. Somerset Maugham Collection. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: This finding aid was produced from previously existing cards in the Manuscripts Catalog, or from another inventory. All pertinent bibliographical information has been retained. If the collection also contains unlisted or unsorted material, it has been noted in the box-and-folder list. This finding aid will be updated periodically to account for new materials that may be added to the collection. The date of the most recent update is noted on the title page. For information on material that may have been added since the last update, please consult the Public Service Desk.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This finding aid encompasses accessions of single manuscripts and small groups of manuscripts and other papers, by or pertaining to W. Somerset Maugham, which the library has chosen to group and list as an artificial collection.

title.part: Lady Frederick: a comedy in three acts

did.unittitle: Letters from W. Somerset Maugham
45 Letters and cards to Lady Marion Bateman

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Accompanied by one photograph and one clipping. Gift, 1971.

did.unittitle: ALS to Ruth Buddington, Villa Mauresque, Cap. Ferrat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: With envelope. Accompanied by carbon copy of TL from Ruth L. Buddington (later Ruth B. Leighton] to Maugham, 1935 Aug 24. Gift, 1963.

did.unittitle: 3 ALS and 1 ANS to W. Morris Colles
ALS to [William Morris] Colles, Westminster
ALS to F. Tennyson Jesse, St. Jean Cap Ferrat
38 ALS and 1 APS to Mrs. Ada Leverson
ALS to [Benjamin Moore-Fine], Villa Mauresque, Cap Ferrat
2 TLS to Miss Morse of the George H. Doran Company of New York
2 TLS to [John W.] Rumsey, the first with an autograph note at the end
4 ALS and 7 TLS to John W. Rumsey, various places
TLS to "Charlie" [Charles Hanson Towne], Bryanston Square, [London]
10 TLS to Charles Hanson Towne; also 7 ALS with a typescript of each letter, various places
TLS to Mr. Wilensky, Villa Mauresque, Cap Ferrat
ALS to Donald G. Wing, San Francisco
TLS to Donald Goddard Wing, St. Jean-Cap Ferrat
TLS to Donald Goddard Wing, St. Jean-Cap Ferrat

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This letter refers to his manuscript of "On a Chinese Screen," which he gave to the Yale Library in 1954. Gift, 1954.

did.unittitle: Writings of W. Somerset Maugham
"Ashenden"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Typescript with a few autograph manuscript suggestions. Gift, 1951.

did.unittitle: "The Extraordinary Sex," proof, signed
"Four Dutchmen," proof, signed
"In Hiding," proof, signed
"Lady Frederick", New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A play in typescript. An acting copy. 3 acts. Gift, 1958.

did.unittitle: "The Man Who Made His Mark," proof, signed
"Mrs. Dot," New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: A play in typescript. Two acting copies. Gift, 1958.

did.unittitle: "On a Chinese Screen," London

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript. Several pages written on both sides. Bound in purple leather. Gift, 1954.

did.unittitle: "Straight Flush," proof, signed
"Strictly Personal"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Autograph manuscript, with many autograph corrections and deletions. Accompanied by a TLS to Mr. Knollenberg, 1942 Sep 28, New York. Gift, 1942.

did.unittitle: Other
For "The Explorer." Between Maugham and Lewis Waller of the Lyric Theatre, London
For "Eliza of Lambeth." Between Maugham and Thomas Fisher Unwin of London
For "The Making of a Saint." Between Maugham and Thomas Fisher Unwin of London
For "The Merry-Go-Round." Between Maugham and the firm of William Heinemann of London
Artwork
Dyson, William Henry. Etching of Hugh Walpole hitting Eleanor Mordaunt in the jaw

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entitled "The manly art of self-defence or "Now no one can say that Maugham meant it for me!" Gift, 1959.

did.unittitle: Oversize
"The Extraordinary Sex," proof, signed (in Series II)
"Four Dutchmen," proof, signed (in Series II)
"In Hiding," proof, signed (in Series II)
"The Man Who Made His Mark," proof, signed (in Series II)
"Straight Flush," proof, signed (in Series II)
Dyson, William Henry. Etching of Hugh Walpole hitting Eleanor Mordaunt in the jaw (in Series III)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Entitled "The manly art of self-defence or "Now no one can say that Maugham meant it for me!" Gift, 1959.

did.unittitle: Additions Since June 1997
MAUGHAM, W. SOMERSET
"Introduction" to Moll Flanders by Daniel Defoe

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: 1 item (8 leaves) ; 28 cm. Holograph, corrected. Purchase, 1997.
